title: 10 best herbal capsules for chronic bronchitis
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for chronic bronchitis are dietary supplements that contain a blend of herbs and plant extracts, formulated to alleviate symptoms and manage the condition. These natural remedies often include ingredients like thyme, ivy leaf, and butterbar root, which have anti-inflammatory, expectorant, and soothing properties that help reduce inflammation, thin mucus, and relieve coughing spells. By incorporating herbal capsules into their treatment plan, individuals with chronic bronchitis can experience improved lung function, fewer symptoms, and an overall better quality of life. For instance, a study published in the Journal of Herbal Medicine found that patients who took a thyme-ivy leaf combination experienced significant improvements in coughing and mucus production compared to those on a placebo.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme capsule helps with chronic bronchitis because it contains active compounds such as thymol, carvacrol, and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant properties. These compounds help reduce inflammation in the airways, relieve coughing and ease breathing. Additionally, thyme has been shown to have antibacterial and antiviral effects, which may help prevent or treat respiratory infections that can exacerbate chronic bronchitis. By promoting the expulsion of mucus and reducing inflammation, thyme capsules can help alleviate symptoms of chronic bronchitis and improve overall lung function.
remedy_properties: Expectorant: Thyme capsules help loosen and remove mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe, due to the active constituents thymol and carvacrol.
Antitussive: Thyme capsules can help reduce coughing fits, providing relief for chronic bronchitis patients who experience persistent coughing, thanks to the compound flavonoids.
Antibacterial: Thyme capsules can help fight bacterial infections in the respiratory tract, which can exacerbate chronic bronchitis symptoms, due to the presence of thymol and other volatile oils.

Anti-inflammatory: Thyme capsules can help reduce inflammation in the airways, alleviating chronic bronchitis symptoms, as a result of the active constituents, including rosmarinic acid and ursolic acid.
Bronchodilatory: Thyme capsules can help widen the airways, improving airflow and making breathing easier for chronic bronchitis patients, due to the active compound thymol.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are rich in flavonoids and volatile oils, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to alleviate chronic bronchitis symptoms.
Flowers: Thyme flowers contain essential oils and bioactive compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, that have been shown to reduce inflammation and mucus production in the respiratory tract.
Stems: Although less potent than leaves and flowers, thyme stems still contain beneficial compounds that can help soothe irritated airways and reduce inflammation associated with chronic bronchitis.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 tablespoons of dried thyme leaves and 1/2 cup of hot water; steep thyme leaves in hot water for 10-15 minutes to create a strong thyme tea.
Strain the tea to remove the leaves, leaving behind a potent thyme liquid; let the liquid cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Mix 2-3 teaspoons of powdered gelatin with 2 tablespoons of the cooled thyme tea, stirring until a gel-like consistency forms.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the thyme-gelatin mixture, using a capsule-filling tool; allow the capsules to dry for 24-48 hours.
Take 1-2 thyme capsules, 2-3 times a day, as a natural remedy for chronic bronchitis, following the advice of a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eastern purple coneflower capsule helps with chronic bronchitis because it contains high levels of echinacea, an active ingredient known for its anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties. Chronic bronchitis is characterized by inflammation and irritation of the bronchial tubes, leading to coughing and mucus production. The echinacea in eastern purple coneflower capsules works to reduce inflammation, soothe irritation, and strengthen the immune system, helping to alleviate symptoms of chronic bronchitis. Additionally, echinacea has been shown to have antiviral and antibacterial effects, which may help to prevent respiratory infections that can exacerbate chronic bronchitis. Overall, eastern purple coneflower capsules can provide relief and support for those suffering from chronic bronchitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eastern purple coneflower capsules can reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, alleviating symptoms of chronic bronchitis by decreasing irritation and swelling in the airways. This property is due to the presence of chlorogenic acid and caffeic acid.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of eastern purple coneflower capsules help protect lung tissue from oxidative stress and damage caused by chronic bronchitis, promoting overall respiratory health. The antioxidant activity is attributed to phenolic acids, flavonoids, and other polyphenols.

Antimicrobial: Eastern purple coneflower capsules have antimicrobial properties that can help prevent or reduce respiratory infections, which is essential for managing chronic bronchitis. The antimicrobial effects are primarily due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, such as eudesmanolides and germacranolides.

Immunomodulatory: The immunomodulatory properties of eastern purple coneflower capsules help regulate and balance the immune system, supporting the body's ability to manage chronic bronchitis by reducing inflammation and preventing infections. These properties are attributed to polysaccharides and other compounds that influence immune cell function.

Expectorant: Eastern purple coneflower capsules can help loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier for individuals with chronic bronchitis to breathe. This expectorant effect is due to the presence of volatile oils and other compounds that stimulate mucus secretion and clearance.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in flavonoids, flowers of eastern purple coneflower are used in teas to help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, providing relief for chronic bronchitis.
Leaves: High in antioxidants, coneflower leaves are used in teas to support the immune system and soothe irritated airways in individuals with chronic bronchitis.
Stems: Containing rosmarinic acid, coneflower stems are used in teas to alleviate cough and reduce mucus production, helping manage chronic bronchitis symptoms.
Roots: Packed with phenolic acids, coneflower roots are used in teas to reduce inflammation and provide antibacterial properties, aiding in the treatment of chronic bronchitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest purple coneflower (Echinacea purpurea) flowers and leaves, ensuring they are fully grown and dry; gently clean any dirt, allowing them to air dry for 24 hours.
Grind dried coneflower herbs into a coarse powder using a mortar and pestle or herb grinder, aiming for a consistency similar to coffee grounds.
Measure out 500mg of the coneflower powder per capsule, using a scale for accuracy, and fill size "0" vegetarian capsules with the powder.
Seal the filled capsules using a capsule machine or by hand, ensuring a tight seal to preserve freshness and potency.
Store the completed coneflower capsules in airtight containers, preferably in a cool, dark location, for up to 12 months, ensuring potency for chronic bronchitis relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: African geranium capsule helps with chronic bronchitis because it contains pelargonium sidoides, a plant extract that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. These properties can help reduce inflammation in the airways, thin and loosen mucus, and make it easier to cough up. Additionally, the herb may help boost the immune system, which can be beneficial in managing chronic bronchitis. Studies have suggested that pelargonium sidoides may help alleviate symptoms such as cough, shortness of breath, and chest pain in people with chronic bronchitis. Furthermore, it may help reduce the frequency and severity of bronchitis episodes. Overall, African geranium capsule can be a natural and effective way to manage chronic bronchitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: African geranium capsules contain active constituents such as flavonoids and triterpenoids that help reduce inflammation in the airways, which is important for chronic bronchitis as it can alleviate symptoms like coughing and difficulty breathing.

Antimicrobial: The presence of essential oils like geraniol and citronellol in African geranium capsules provide antimicrobial properties, helping to fight off infections that can exacerbate chronic bronchitis.

Expectorant: African geranium capsules contain compounds that stimulate the expulsion of mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel mucus and reduce congestion in the airways, which is beneficial for those with chronic bronchitis.

Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content in African geranium capsules, due to compounds like phenolic acids, helps protect lung tissue from oxidative stress and damage, which can help slow the progression of chronic bronchitis.

Immunomodulatory: African geranium capsules can help regulate and strengthen the immune system, which is important for managing chronic bronchitis as it can help the body better fight off infections and reduce inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the African geranium capsule plant are used in medicinal tea for chronic bronchitis due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways.
Flowers: The flowers of the African geranium capsule plant are used in medicinal tea for chronic bronchitis because they contain antioxidants that can help protect the lungs from damage.
Roots: The roots of the African geranium capsule plant are used in medicinal tea for chronic bronchitis due to their expectorant properties, which can help clear mucus from the lungs.
Bark: The bark of the African geranium capsule plant is used in medicinal tea for chronic bronchitis because it contains tannins that can help soothe irritated throat tissue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest African geranium (Pelargonium sidoides) leaves, rinse, and dry for 24 hours in a well-ventilated area.
Grind dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Measure 500mg of the powdered herb for each capsule, using a scale.
Fill size "0" gelatin or vegetarian capsules with the measured powder.
Store capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hyssop capsule helps with chronic bronchitis because it contains antispasmodic and expectorant properties that alleviate symptoms of the condition. The herb's natural oils and flavonoids work to soothe the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and relaxing the muscles in the airways. As a result, it becomes easier to expel mucus and breathe. Furthermore, hyssop has been shown to have antibacterial and antiviral effects, which may help prevent the recurrence of bronchitis infections. Its ability to stimulate the immune system also aids in the body's defense against respiratory ailments.
remedy_properties: Expectorant: Hyssop capsules help to loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, providing relief from congestion and making it easier to breathe, due to the presence of volatile oils such as pinocamphone and thujone.
Antispasmodic: Hyssop capsules can help to relax the smooth muscles of the respiratory tract, reducing coughing and spasms, thanks to the compound marrubiin.

Anti-inflammatory: Hyssop capsules may help to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, alleviating symptoms of chronic bronchitis, as a result of the flavonoids and phenolic acids found in the plant.

Antimicrobial: Hyssop capsules possess antimicrobial properties that may help to combat infections contributing to chronic bronchitis, due to the presence of volatile oils like pinocamphone and camphene.

Antioxidant: Hyssop capsules contain antioxidants that can help protect the respiratory tract from damage caused by free radicals, potentially slowing the progression of chronic bronchitis, owing to the high levels of flavonoids and phenolic acids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the hyssop capsule plant are used to make medicinal tea for chronic bronchitis due to their expectorant and soothing properties, which help to clear mucus and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract.

Flowers: The flowers of the hyssop capsule plant are used in medicinal tea for chronic bronchitis due to their antispasmodic and calming effects, which help to alleviate coughing and soothe irritated airways.

Stems: The stems of the hyssop capsule plant are used in medicinal tea for chronic bronchitis as they contain essential oils and other compounds that have been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce inflammation and fight infections in the respiratory tract.

Seeds: The seeds of the hyssop capsule plant are used in medicinal tea for chronic bronchitis due to their high concentration of antioxidants, which help to protect the respiratory tract from damage caused by free radicals and reduce oxidative stress.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried hyssop leaves and flowers, and grind them in a spice grinder for 1-2 minutes until they form a fine powder.
Mix the ground hyssop with 2 teaspoons of a binding agent, such as tapioca starch or gelatin, in a small bowl for 30 seconds until a uniform consistency is achieved.
Using a capsule-filling machine or by hand, fill size "00" empty gelatin capsules with the hyssop-binding agent mixture, making sure not to overfill them.
Allow the filled capsules to dry at room temperature for 2-3 hours before storing them in a tightly sealed container.
Take one to two capsules three times a day, 30 minutes before meals, for a period of 4-6 weeks, or as directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cowslip capsule helps with chronic bronchitis because it contains anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that are beneficial for respiratory health. The herb's mucilage content soothes irritated mucous membranes, reducing inflammation in the airways and making it easier to expel mucus. Additionally, cowslip stimulates the production of healthy respiratory secretions, which helps clear congestion and alleviate coughing. Its antispasmodic properties further help to relax the muscles in the respiratory tract, providing relief from bronchial spasms and shortness of breath. Overall, cowslip capsules offer a natural and effective way to manage chronic bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Expectorant: Cowslip capsules help to loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to cough up and clear the airways, which is important for chronic bronchitis as it helps to reduce inflammation and congestion. This property is due to the saponins and mucilage present in cowslip.

Anti-inflammatory: Cowslip capsules have been shown to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can help alleviate symptoms of chronic bronchitis such as coughing and shortness of breath. This property is attributed to the flavonoids and saponins found in cowslip.

Antispasmodic: Cowslip capsules can help to relax and soothe the muscles of the respiratory tract, reducing the frequency and severity of coughing fits, which is beneficial for chronic bronchitis as it helps to prevent further damage to the airways. This property is due to the alkaloids and saponins present in cowslip.

Antimicrobial: Cowslip capsules have antimicrobial properties that can help to fight off respiratory infections, which is important for chronic bronchitis as it can help to prevent the progression of the disease. This property is attributed to the essential oils and tannins found in cowslip.

Diuretic: Cowslip capsules have a diuretic effect, which can help to remove excess fluid from the lungs and reduce the workload on the respiratory system. This property is due to the saponins and flavonoids present in cowslip.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, cowslip flowers help soothe irritated bronchial tubes and alleviate chronic bronchitis symptoms.
Leaves: Containing mucilage and saponins, cowslip leaves help reduce inflammation, thin mucus, and ease expectoration in chronic bronchitis patients.
Roots: With expectorant and antispasmodic properties, cowslip roots aid in clearing phlegm and calming coughing spasms in individuals with chronic bronchitis.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 teaspoons of dried cowslip flowers and leaves, ensuring they are organically grown and free from pesticides.
Grind the cowslip flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 2-3 minutes.
Mix the ground cowslip powder with 1-2 teaspoons of a binding agent, such as natural gum arabic or powdered slippery elm, until thoroughly combined.
Fill size "00" empty gelatin or vegetarian capsules with the herbal mixture, using a capsule filling machine or by hand, ensuring each capsule contains an equal amount of the powder.

Seal the capsules and store in a cool, dry, and dark place for up to six months, taking 1-2 capsules, three times a day, as a dietary supplement for chronic bronchitis. Consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new supplement regimen.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Verbena capsule helps with chronic bronchitis because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. These properties work to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, verbena can help to loosen and expel mucus from the lungs, further alleviating symptoms of chronic bronchitis. Its soothing effects can also help to calm coughing spasms and reduce irritation in the throat. Furthermore, verbena has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent infections that can exacerbate chronic bronchitis. Overall, verbena capsules can provide relief from the symptoms of chronic bronchitis and support respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Verbena capsules can reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, helping alleviate symptoms of chronic bronchitis by decreasing inflammation-induced airway obstruction and mucus production. The active constituents responsible for this property include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and verbascoside.

Antitussive: Verbena has been shown to help suppress coughing, which is a common symptom of chronic bronchitis. This property is important as it can provide relief to patients and potentially prevent further irritation and damage to the respiratory tract. The active constituents responsible for this property include verbascoside and various essential oils.

Expectorant: Verbena capsules can help loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier for individuals with chronic bronchitis to breathe. This property is crucial for managing the disease, as mucus buildup can obstruct airways and exacerbate symptoms. The active constituents responsible for this property include iridoid glycosides and essential oils.

Antioxidant: Verbena contains antioxidants that can help protect the respiratory tract from oxidative stress and damage, which are often associated with chronic bronchitis. This property is important for overall respiratory health and may help reduce the progression of chronic bronchitis. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and verbascoside.

Antimicrobial: Verbena has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent or reduce respiratory infections, which are common in individuals with chronic bronchitis. This property is important for managing chronic bronchitis, as infections can worsen symptoms and lead to complications. The active constituents responsible for this property include essential oils, flavonoids, and iridoid glycosides.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to alleviate symptoms of chronic bronchitis.
Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and volatile oils, flowers can help soothe irritated bronchial passages and reduce inflammation.
Roots: Packed with active constituents, roots can help clear mucus from the respiratory tract and provide relief from persistent coughing.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 tablespoons of dried lemon verbena leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Measure out 0.5-1 teaspoon of the verbena powder and mix it with an equal amount of bulking agent, such as tapioca starch or rice flour.
Fill size 00 vegetarian capsules with the herbal mixture using a capsule filling machine or by hand.
Store the completed capsules in a cool, dry place away from direct sunlight.
Take 1-2 capsules, three times daily, as needed for chronic bronchitis symptoms, or as directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Indian tobacco capsule helps with chronic bronchitis because it contains natural herbs that have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. These herbs work to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe and expel mucus. Additionally, the herbs found in the capsule can help to soothe irritated throat and lung tissue, providing relief from coughing and other symptoms associated with chronic bronchitis. The natural ingredients in the capsule also work to boost the immune system, which can help to prevent further infections and flare-ups. Overall, the herbal Indian tobacco capsule can help to alleviate symptoms of chronic bronchitis and improve lung function, making it easier for individuals to manage their condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Indian tobacco capsules contain active constituents like lobeline and pyridine alkaloids, which help reduce inflammation in the airways, a key symptom of chronic bronchitis.

Expectorant: The herb contains saponins and essential oils that have expectorant properties, helping to loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, alleviating congestion in chronic bronchitis.
Bronchodilator: Lobeline in Indian tobacco acts as a bronchodilator, relaxing bronchial smooth muscles and improving breathing, which is beneficial for individuals with chronic bronchitis.
Antitussive: The antitussive properties of Indian tobacco, due to the presence of various alkaloids, help suppress chronic coughing, a common symptom of chronic bronchitis.

Antimicrobial: Indian tobacco has antimicrobial properties, primarily due to the presence of alkaloids and flavonoids, which help protect the respiratory tract from infections, a common complication in chronic bronchitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate symptoms of chronic bronchitis.
Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and other beneficial compounds, which can help soothe irritated throat and improve respiratory function, making them useful for treating chronic bronchitis.
Roots: Have expectorant and demulcent properties, which can help loosen phlegm and soothe irritated mucous membranes, providing relief for individuals with chronic bronchitis.
Bark: Contains tannins and other compounds that have astringent properties, which can help reduce inflammation and soothe irritated airways, providing relief for those with chronic bronchitis.
remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients: 500mg of holy basil leaf, 500mg of turmeric root, 500mg of ginger root, and size 00 vegetarian capsules.
Grind the holy basil leaf, turmeric root, and ginger root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground herbs and mix thoroughly.
Fill size 00 vegetarian capsules with 500mg of the herbal mixture using a capsule filling machine.
Store the completed capsules in a cool, dry place and take one capsule up to three times daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elecampane capsule helps with chronic bronchitis because it contains substances that help reduce inflammation in the airways and improve breathing. The herb's active compounds, such as alantolactone and isoalantolactone, have been shown to have expectorant, antitussive, and antibacterial properties. These properties help to clear mucus from the lungs, soothe coughs, and fight off infections, making it easier to breathe and reducing the severity of chronic bronchitis symptoms. Additionally, elecampane has been used traditionally to strengthen the respiratory system, making it a valuable herbal remedy for those suffering from chronic bronchitis.
remedy_properties: Expectorant: Elecampane capsules help clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe, due to its high content of volatile oils like alantolactone and isoalantolactone.
Anti-inflammatory: The plant's ability to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract helps alleviate irritation and swelling, and this is attributed mainly to its sesquiterpene lactone content.

Antimicrobial: Elecampane capsules can help fight off respiratory infections, a common issue in chronic bronchitis, thanks to the presence of antimicrobial compounds such as alantolactone and isoalantolactone.

Antitussive: Elecampane capsules can help suppress coughing, reducing discomfort and fatigue for chronic bronchitis patients, due to the presence of alantolactone and its sedative effects on the cough reflex.

Immune-boosting: Elecampane capsules can strengthen the immune system, making it easier for the body to fight off chronic bronchitis infections, due to its polysaccharide content that stimulates the immune system.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of elecampane are commonly used in making medicinal tea for chronic bronchitis due to their expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help clear mucus from the respiratory tract.

Rhizomes: Elecampane's rhizomes are rich in beneficial compounds like inulin and sesquiterpene lactones, making them useful for soothing coughs and easing breathing difficulties associated with chronic bronchitis.
Flowers: Elecampane flowers contain flavonoids and essential oils that have been found to have calming effects on the respiratory system, making them helpful in managing chronic bronchitis symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves of elecampane are a source of antioxidants that can help reduce inflammation and support overall respiratory health in individuals with chronic bronchitis.
Seeds: Elecampane seeds are not commonly used in medicinal tea, but they do contain beneficial oils that can help alleviate congestion and promote relaxation in the respiratory system.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 teaspoons of dried elecampane root and grind it into a fine powder, which should take around 5 minutes.
Measure out 0.5-1 teaspoon of the elecampane root powder and mix it with an equal amount of a bulking agent, such as tapioca starch or rice flour.
Fill size "0" empty gelatin or vegetarian capsules with the elecampane-bulking agent mixture, using a capsule filling machine or by hand.
Store the completed elecampane capsules in a cool, dry place, away from direct sunlight.
Take 1-2 capsules, three times a day, as needed for chronic bronchitis, following the advice of a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Malabar nut capsule helps with chronic bronchitis because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. These properties help to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, malabar nut can help to loosen and expel mucus from the lungs, which can reduce the severity and frequency of coughing fits. Furthermore, malabar nut has been shown to have antibacterial and antiviral properties, which can help to protect the respiratory system from infections that can exacerbate chronic bronchitis. Overall, malabar nut capsules can help to alleviate the symptoms of chronic bronchitis and improve lung function.
remedy_properties: Expectorant: Malabar nut capsules help clear mucus and reduce coughing by stimulating the production of sputum, making it easier to expel mucus from the respiratory tract. This is due to the presence of alkaloids such as ajmalicine and serpentine.

Anti-inflammatory: The herb has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, alleviating symptoms of bronchitis. The active constituents responsible for this property include tannins, saponins, and flavonoids.

Antimicrobial: Malabar nut capsules have antimicrobial properties that aid in fighting infections caused by bacteria, viruses, and fungi, which are common in chronic bronchitis. The antimicrobial activity is attributed to the presence of alkaloids, tannins, and saponins.

Immunomodulatory: The herb helps regulate and strengthen the immune system, enabling the body to fight off infections and manage chronic bronchitis more effectively. This is due to the presence of various phytochemicals, such as phenols, terpenoids, and steroids.

Antioxidant: Malabar nut capsules contain antioxidants that help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which are common in chronic bronchitis. The antioxidant properties are attributed to the presence of flavonoids, tannins, and phenolic acids.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the malabar nut capsule plant are used to make medicinal tea for chronic bronchitis due to their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the airways and expel mucus.

Leaves: The leaves of the malabar nut capsule plant are used in medicinal tea for chronic bronchitis because they contain compounds with antibacterial and antiviral properties, which can help to fight infections and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract.

Seeds: The seeds of the malabar nut capsule plant are used in medicinal tea for chronic bronchitis due to their expectorant and mucolytic properties, which help to break up and expel mucus from the lungs.

Bark: The bark of the malabar nut capsule plant is used in medicinal tea for chronic bronchitis because it contains tannins and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce inflammation and protect against damage in the respiratory tract.
remedy_recipe: Gather 400 mg of dried Malabar nut (Adhatoda vasica) leaves and 50 mg of granulated microcrystalline cellulose as a binding agent.
Grind the Malabar nut leaves into a fine powder using a clean, dry electric coffee grinder, approximately 3-5 minutes.
Combine the ground Malabar nut leaves and microcrystalline cellulose in a small mixing bowl, and stir until evenly distributed, about 1-2 minutes.
Fill size "00" empty gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture using a capsule filling machine or by hand, ensuring consistent dosage of 400 mg per capsule.
Seal the filled capsules and store in a cool, dry, and dark place, away from direct sunlight, for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elderberry capsule helps with chronic bronchitis because it is rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals that support the immune system and reduce inflammation. The anti-inflammatory properties of elderberry can help alleviate symptoms of chronic bronchitis, such as coughing and chest congestion. Elderberry also contains antiviral and antibacterial compounds that can help fight off infections and reduce the duration of respiratory illnesses. Additionally, elderberry has been shown to have expectorant properties, which can help loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract. By supporting the immune system and reducing inflammation, elderberry capsules can help manage the symptoms of chronic bronchitis and promote overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Elderberry capsules can help reduce inflammation in the airways, alleviating symptoms of chronic bronchitis by potentially decreasing irritation and swelling. This property is due to the presence of anthocyanins and other flavonoids.

Antioxidant: Elderberry capsules contain high levels of antioxidants, which can help protect the respiratory system from oxidative stress and damage, supporting overall lung health in chronic bronchitis patients. The antioxidant activity comes from anthocyanins, flavonols, and phenolic acids.

Immunomodulatory: Elderberry capsules can help strengthen the immune system, enabling the body to better fight off infections and reduce the frequency and severity of chronic bronchitis exacerbations. This is attributed to the presence of lectins, flavonoids, and anthocyanins.

Antiviral: Elderberry capsules have been shown to have antiviral properties, which can help prevent or reduce the duration of respiratory viral infections, including those that can trigger chronic bronchitis symptoms. The antiviral activity is primarily due to the presence of the lectin, Sambucus nigra agglutinin (SNA).

Expectorant: Elderberry capsules can help loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier for individuals with chronic bronchitis to breathe and reducing the risk of secondary infections. This expectorant effect is attributed to the high content of flavonoids, particularly quercetin and rutin.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in flavonoids, elderberry flowers help reduce inflammation and congestion in the respiratory tract, providing relief for chronic bronchitis.
Berries: High in antioxidants and vitamins, elderberry seeds and skin extracts have been shown to boost the immune system and reduce the duration of respiratory infections.
Leaves: Containing tannins and mucilage, elderberry leaves can help soothe irritated mucous membranes and provide a calming effect on the respiratory tract.
Bark: With anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, elderberry bark can help alleviate cough and clear mucus from the airways, offering relief for chronic bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried elderberries and 1 teaspoon of cinnamon powder, then combine them in a bowl.
In a saucepan, boil 2 cups of water, then add the elderberry-cinnamon mixture, reduce heat, and simmer for 30 minutes.
After simmering, let the mixture cool down, then strain it using a cheesecloth into a clean bowl.
Mix the strained liquid with 1 cup of vegetable capsule powder, then fill size 00 gelatin capsules with the mixture.
Allow the filled capsules to dry for 24 hours before storing in an airtight container; take 2 capsules, 3 times a day, for chronic bronchitis relief.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat chronic bronchitis with herbal capsules is through the use of natural anti-inflammatory and expectorant herbs. Look for a product containing standardized extracts of herbs such as licorice root, thyme, and ivy leaf. These herbs can help reduce inflammation in the airways, thin mucus, and make it easier to cough up. Always consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new supplement regimen, especially if you have a chronic condition or are taking other medications.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to chronic bronchitis that are treated with herbal capsules include respiratory conditions such as asthma, bronchial inflammation, and recurring bronchitis. Herbal remedies often contain a mix of natural ingredients like plant extracts, which can help alleviate symptoms like coughing, chest tightness, and mucus production. For instance, capsules with thyme or elecampane have been used to soothe respiratory tract inflammation and improve expectoration. However, it's crucial to consult with a healthcare professional before starting any herbal treatment to ensure safety, effectiveness, and appropriate dosage.
